results.title: Mem 2.0 Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

•1 review

If you're constantly taking notes and are overwhelmed by the volume, then you need an app to capture them all without having to worry about the organization process. That's what Mem does really well. Mem's features make it easy to find, clarify, cross reference and combine your information so you can start making it useful. Mem behaves like an interactive second brain more than any other tool I've tried. The more info you give it, the more it can help you as a personal assistant. Once Mem has OCR features for text, it will become indispensable for me. Its a great app so please give it a try.

#### What's great

AI assistant (4)note-taking (7)search (5)

#### What needs improvement

OCR missing (1)

Report

•1 review

I’ve tried almost every note app out there, but Mem 2.0 is the one that finally feels frictionless. The backslash formatting is so intuitive that I actually enjoy organizing my thoughts instead of fighting the interface. The search and autotagging features make it ridiculously easy to find what I need without spending time sorting. Plus, the Chrome extension is a game changer for saving articles and webpages for later. Mem just gets out of your way and lets you think.

Top reviewed

Across the leading LLM memory products, patterns split between developer infrastructure, personal knowledge recall, and workflow continuity. Supermemory emphasizes enterprise-grade persistent context and multimodal retrieval, Mem 2.0 focuses on AI-assisted note capture and resurfacing, while OpenMemory MCP represents lightweight private memory for tool-based sessions and cross-session continuity.

Agentmemory

Agentmemory

Maker

📌

Hey Product Hunt 👋

I built AgentMemory because coding agents still have one painful limitation: they forget between sessions.

 You explain your architecture once.
 You debug a production issue once.
 You decide on a library or pattern once.

Then the next session starts from zero again. [...] Subscribe

Agentmemory

## Persistent memory for Claude Code, Codex & coding agents

574 followers

## Persistent memory for Claude Code, Codex & coding agents

574 followers

Visit website

AI Infrastructure Tools

•

LLM Developer Tools

You can now give Hermes, Claude Code, and Codex infinite memory. Agentmemory is trending on GitHub with 5,000+ Stars. CLAUDE md dumps 22,000+ tokens into context at 240 observations agentmemory: 1,900 tokens. same observations. 92% less. At 1,000 observations, 80% of your built-in memories become invisible. agentmemory keeps 100% searchable. benchmarked on 240 real coding sessions → Up to 95% fewer tokens per session → 200x more tool calls before hitting context limits → 100% open source

 Overview
 Reviews
 Alternatives
 Forum
 Team
 Awards [...] Then the next session starts from zero again.

AgentMemory gives AI coding agents persistent memory across sessions, so they can actually build on what they’ve already learned about your codebase. It automatically captures what your agent does, compresses it into structured memories, indexes them with hybrid search, and injects the right context back into future sessions.

It works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Kilo Code, OpenCode, Cline, Roo, Goose, Aider, Hermes, OpenClaw, and basically any MCP or REST-capable agent.

From day one, I wanted it to be:

 100% open source
 Free to run locally
 No external database required
 Works via MCP, REST, and simple hooks
 Built for real coding workflows, not toy “chat history” memory
